name: Bob Curnow
abstract: Robert Harry Curnow (born November 1, 1941) is an American musician who served as a trombonist, staff arranger and producer for the Stan Kenton Orchestra during the 1960s and 1970s. As a composer and arranger, he has become well known for large ensemble jazz music set to contemporary fusion and rock music of groups such as Chicago, Blood…

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: I had sent a few old charts to Bob to do. He did a good job with them, good distribution, good promotion, and everything. Sold quite a few of them. So I decided to give him all the new ones too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
